channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Presidency Denies Alleged Poison Plot By Aso Rock Kitchen Staff
item.description: The Presidency don clarify say all those tori wey dey circulate for social media about kitchen staff for Aso Rock trying to poison President Bola Tinubu na pure false news. Bayo Onanuga, wey dey serve...

item.title: Marjorie Taylor Greene Criticizes White House Over Hypothetical Military Draft Remarks
item.description: Former Congresswoman Marjorie Taylor Greene reacted strongly after White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt appeared on Fox News’ Sunday Morning Futures. During the interview, host Maria Bartiromo...

item.title: Rising Ethiopia-Eritrea Tensions Put Egypt in Difficult Strategic Position
item.description: Three years after Ethiopia and Eritrea fought together against the Tigray People’s Liberation Front (TPLF) in the Tigray war, the two former allies are now moving closer to confrontation again. Addis ...

item.title: Peter Obi, Senators Attend ADC Membership Registration and Revalidation in Anambra
item.description: Peter Obi, former governor of Anambra State and 2023 Labour Party presidential candidate, join forces with Senators Victor Umeh and Tony Nwoye for the African Democratic Congress (ADC) membership regi...

item.title: Matildas Draw with South Korea, Face Tougher Path to Asian Cup Quarter-Finals
item.description: The Matildas secured a 3-3 draw against South Korea in their final Group A match at the Women's Asian Cup, but it was a result that complicated their route to the quarter-finals. Despite two earlier w...

item.title: Health Officials Say Trace Chemicals in Sanitary Pads Pose No Proven Harm
item.description: Health minister Aaron Motsoaledi has urged the public not to panic over recent findings of endocrine-disrupting chemicals (EDCs) in certain sanitary pads and panty-liners sold in South Africa. During ...

item.title: Jane Mugoh Advises Single Mothers to Choose Supportive Partners on International Women’s Day
item.description: On International Women's Day, private detective Jane Mugoh shared her personal experiences and reflections on love and marriage, particularly addressing single mothers. Speaking to TUKO.co.ke, she rev...

item.title: Iran Appoints Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader Following His Father’s Death
item.description: Iran’s Assembly of Experts has officially named Mojtaba Khamenei as the country’s new supreme leader, succeeding his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was recently assassinated after US and Israeli ...

item.title: Iran's Assembly of Experts Names Mojtaba Khamenei as Successor Amid Ongoing Conflict
item.description: In a pivotal development amid escalating conflict with the United States and Israel, Iran's Assembly of Experts has reportedly selected Mojtaba Khamenei, the second son of the late Supreme Leader Ayat...

item.title: NHRC and CISLAC Call for Increased Women Participation in Nigerian Politics
item.description: For Nigeria, we get over 104 million women but dem still dey underrepresented for political and leadership positions. As we dey celebrate International Women’s Day 2026, the National Human Rights Comm...

item.title: Federal Ministry of Environment Validates Policy Brief on Marine Litter Prevention to Support Circular Economy Transition
item.description: The Federal Ministry of Environment hold meeting with important stakeholders for one validation workshop in Abuja to check and approve one important policy document wey go fight marine litter and help...

item.title: Train services delayed due to fire near Glasgow Central Station
item.description: A fire has broken out near Glasgow Central Station, causing significant disruptions to train services in and around the area. Emergency services were promptly dispatched to contain the blaze, and loca...

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei Appointed Iran's Supreme Leader Amid Escalating Iran-Israel-US Conflict
item.description: On 8th March 2026, Iran's Assembly of Experts appointed Mojtaba Khamenei, the second son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as the new Supreme Leader following the death of his father in U.S.-Israeli strikes....

item.title: Allegations of Religious Messaging to U.S. Troops Regarding Iran Conflict
item.description: According to reports compiled by the Military Religious Freedom Foundation (MRFF), over 100 complaints from U.S. troops suggest that some military commanders have been promoting a religious narrative ...

item.title: Police reveal facial reconstruction of unidentified man found in Welsh reservoir
item.description: In October 2024, a dog walker discovered the body of a man wearing a wetsuit, face down, in the Claerwen reservoir in Mid Wales. Despite extensive investigations over the past 16 months—including sear...

item.title: Kenyan Politicians’ Private Planes Highlight Misplaced Priorities
item.description: Kenyan politicians are increasingly using private planes to move around, avoiding the terrible traffic in towns and bad rural roads. While they flaunt expensive homes and choppers, ordinary Kenyans st...

item.title: SANDF to Deploy Troops with R823 Million Budget to Address Gang Violence and Illegal Mining
item.description: The South African National Defence Force (SANDF) is set to deploy troops to Gauteng, the Eastern Cape, and the Western Cape, with the Free State and North West recently added, to combat escalating gan...

item.title: Explosive Device Thrown Near New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s Home During Protests, Suspects Arrested
item.description: On the 7th of March, 2026, during protests outside Gracie Mansion, home of New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani, a homemade explosive device was thrown but failed to detonate. The device, described as a...

item.title: Photographer Brad Rimmer Documents the Decline of Wheatbelt Community Halls in WA
item.description: Over the last two decades, photographer Brad Rimmer has traversed Western Australia’s wheatbelt, noticing a significant decline in the region’s small community halls. Once vital gathering spots for is...

item.title: French Ambassador Defends CFA Franc Benefits for Francophone Nations Including AES Members
item.description: The French Ambassador to Nigeria, Marc Fonbaustier, don talk say the CFA Franc still get plenty use for Francophone African countries, even for the AES countries wey dey try distance themselves from F...
